channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on millions of low-value packages from China
item.description: President Donald Trump has paused the imposition of tariffs on small-value packages arriving from China.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: President Trump says 'we will have relations with North Korea'; it's a 'big asset' that he gets along with Kim
item.description: President Trump met with Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba at the White House and touted his good relationship with Kim Jong Un from meetings in his first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas names 3 more Israeli hostages to be freed as ceasefire deal stays on track
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Hamas identified three more Israeli hostages it plans to free as part of the fragile ceasefire agreement, a sign the deal was moving forward Friday even as U.S. and Israeli officials continued calls to relocate Gaza’s population after the war.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: We will have relations with North Korea
item.description: President Trump on Friday said the U.S. will have relations with North Korea, touting his personal relationship with Kim Jong Un, who rules the nuclear-armed country with an iron fist and has soldiers fighting on the front lines of Russia’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A look at the hostages still held by Hamas in Gaza, by the numbers
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Hamas are in the midst of a six-week ceasefire during which Hamas is releasing dozens of the hostages captured in its Oct. 7, 2023, attack in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump sanctions on ICC protects US military and government officials, former NSC staffer says
item.description: Amid the International Criminal Court’s issuance of arrest warrants for Israeli leaders for their role in waging a self-defense war against Hamas, President Donald Trump punished the global body, prompting strong reactions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: São Paulo, Brazil small plane crash kills 2
item.description: A small plane crashed into a bus on a road in São Paulo, Brazil, on Friday, killing the pilot and co-pilot and injuring two people on the ground, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Autocratic regimes 'relishing' USAID gutting: Samantha Power
item.description: Samantha Power, a former administrator of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), says autocratic regimes across the world are “relishing” the Trump administration's efforts to gut the agency. “It's not even an opinion.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: DOJ disbands foreign influence task force, unit tasked with seizing Russian oligarchs' asset
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi disbanded a Biden-era initiative targeting Russian oligarchs as well as another designed to combat foreign influence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Russia claims it's taken another eastern Ukraine town. Kyiv officials make no comment
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia’s Defense Ministry claimed on Friday that its forces had captured the coal mining town of Toretsk in their latest breakthrough in eastern Ukraine’s Donetsk region, where Ukrainian defenses are creaking.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A small plane loses altitude after takeoff and slams into a Sao Paulo avenue, killing 2 passengers
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A small aircraft crashed on an avenue in Sao Paulo on Friday morning shortly after taking off from a nearby private airport, killing at least two of its passengers, the local firefighter corps said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Frozen US aid brings new challenges to groups hit by harsh crackdowns in Russia and Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Rights groups, activists and independent media in Russia and Belarus endured increased government scrutiny, repressive laws and even being outlawed and forced to operate from exile abroad. Many survived despite the difficulties.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey ends Afghan diplomats' mission, paving way for Taliban to appoint their own team
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey has terminated the mission of Afghan diplomats who were appointed by Afghanistan’s former, pro-Western government, the outgoing diplomatic team said, in a move that paves the way for the Taliban to appoint their own envoys.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Russian spy ship caught fire off Syria's coast, officials say. Here's audio of its broadcasts
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The man is insistent: Our ship is in difficulty, so keep your distance, he instructs another vessel over the radio. “Warship on your course,” he says. "I am drifting. I’m not under command.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Unions sue Trump administration over USAID shutdown
item.description: Unions representing government employees sued the Trump administration Thursday evening to block efforts to shut down the government’s independent foreign assistance agency. Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) launched a full-scale assault on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Historic landmarks in Iraq's Mosul are reopening as the city heals from Islamic State devastation
item.description: MOSUL, Iraq (AP) — For over 850 years, the leaning minaret of the Great Mosque of al-Nuri stood as an iconic landmark in the Iraqi city of Mosul until it was destroyed by the Islamic State group in 2017.
item.dc:creator: STELLA MARTANY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Future looks dire for UN Palestinian refugee agency, says UNRWA chief
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The head of the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees said Thursday that while an Israeli ban has not yet forced the agency to cease operations, it faces an “existential threat” in the long run.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump executive order sanctions international court over Israel war crimes case
item.description: President Trump on Thursday issued an executive order sanctioning the International Criminal Court (ICC) over its pursuit of war crimes cases against Israel’s prime minister and its former defense minister.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy to lead Ukraine’s delegation at Munich Security Conference which JD Vance will attend
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will lead Ukraine’s delegation at the Munich Security Conference next week, which U.S. Vice President J.D.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The sea was once a blessing for the Pakistani city of Gwadar. But it's become a curse
item.description: GWADAR, Pakistan (AP) — There was a time when few people in the coastal Pakistani city of Gwadar understood what climate change was. After a decade of extreme weather, many more do. Rain battered Gwadar for almost 30 consecutive hours last February.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu gifts Trump controversial item that helped turn tide in war against Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu gave President Trump an unusual gift in homage to the controversial pager operation that targeted Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Swedish police say mass shooter was connected to school where he opened fire
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — The shooter who earlier this week killed 10 people in Sweden’s worst mass shooting was connected to the adult education center where he opened fire with at least one rifle-like weapon, law enforcement officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US service member, 3 contractors killed in surveillance mission plane crash in southern Philippines
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — One U.S. service member and three defense contractors were killed Thursday when a plane contracted by the U.S. military crashed in a rice field in the southern Philippines, U.S. Indo-Pacific Command said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military surveillance flight crashes in Philippines, killing 4
item.description: A U.S. military service member and three defense contractors died Thursday in the Philippines after their surveillance flight crashed, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From fighting disease to protecting the Amazon rainforest, USAID has big impact across the globe
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to close the U.S. Agency for International Development has drawn widespread criticism from congressional Democrats and raised questions and concern about the influence billionaire ally Elon Musk wields over the federal government.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and 'no one else' can end the Ukraine-Russia war, US ally says
item.description: Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjártó told Fox News Digital that President Donald Trump has the necessary credibility with both Ukraine and Russia to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Opposition lawmakers protest alleged mistreatment of Indian deportees by US
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — India’s Parliament was disrupted on Thursday as opposition lawmakers protested the alleged mistreatment of 104 Indian immigrants deported by the United States. A U.S.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN and CHONCHUI NGASHANGVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump warns he left instructions to ‘obliterate’ Iran if he is assassinated
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said that he has left instructions that if Iran were to assassination him, the country should be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: White House flags top USAID boondoggles under Elon Musk's microscope
item.description: The White House has released a list of USAID programs its considers wasteful and that it says abused taxpayer funds by backing programs that supported DEI initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation
